description: All drywall hung and screwed, backing and blocking confirmed complete, joints and fasteners ready for tape, and Baldwin County framing/insulation inspection passed before finish work begins.

categories.name: Pre-Hang Verification
items: Baldwin County rough framing inspection passed and card posted on site before first board goes up
All MEP rough-in inspections passed (plumbing, HVAC, electrical) — zero open inspection items remain
Superintendent walk confirms all blocking and backing installed per blocking plan: grab bars, TV mounts, cabinet rails, heavy fixtures, handrails, and towel bars
All exterior walls confirmed insulated and inspected before drywall closes them (R-19 batts minimum or per energy compliance report)
Sound-attenuation insulation installed in all designated walls (home theater, primary suite, bathrooms per plan notes)
Attic and vaulted ceiling insulation complete and inspected prior to drywall on ceiling planes
All recessed can locations marked and trim rings or air-tight IC housings confirmed installed flush to future drywall plane
Fire blocking and draft stopping verified at all penetrations, stair stringers, and top-plate runs exceeding 10 feet per IRC 302.11

categories.name: Materials On-Site
items: Drywall board count and type verified against takeoff: 5/8" Type X in garage, 1/2" standard in living areas, 1/2" moisture-resistant in all wet areas (bathrooms, laundry, mudroom)
Drywall stored flat, off slab on stickers, protected from moisture — no bowed, wet, or delaminated sheets accepted on site
Fastener type confirmed: coarse-thread drywall screws for wood framing, fine-thread for steel; no nails on ceiling planes
Corner bead, control-joint bead, and J-bead quantities on site and matching spec (metal bead required at all outside corners per Palmetto standard — no vinyl bead on $1M+ builds)
Drywall adhesive on hand for glue-and-screw application on all wall boards per superintendent directive
Lift/panel hoist on site and operational before ceiling hang begins

categories.name: Hang Quality
items: Ceiling panels hung perpendicular to joists, glued and screwed at 12" o.c. in field and 8" o.c. at edges — no exceptions on vaulted or tray ceilings
Wall panels hung horizontally (tapered edge out), glued and screwed at 16" o.c. field and 8" o.c. edges; no panel breaks mid-opening on arched or radius walls
All screws driven to dimple — heads set 1/16" below surface without breaking paper; superintendent spot-check minimum one full room per day
No butt joints over windows, door headers, or within 8" of any opening corner — confirmed by superintendent before crew advances room to room
All cutouts (outlets, switches, recessed cans, HVAC registers, plumbing access) clean-cut, sized to box perimeter — no torn paper or blown-out edges
Radius walls, barrel ceilings, and coffered details hung with flex board or scored standard board per architect's detail drawings — no kinking or cracking
1/2" clearance gap maintained at floor on all wall panels to prevent wicking; bottom edge does not contact slab or subfloor

categories.name: Specialty & Code Details
items: Garage-to-living-space walls and ceiling fully hung with 5/8" Type X drywall, all joints and penetrations sealed with fire-rated caulk per IRC 302.5.1
All mechanical room walls and ceilings confirmed with correct fire-rating assembly where required by plan
Shaft walls (HVAC chases, fireplace chases) framed and hung with correct assembly — double-layer or Type X per engineer's detail
Stair soffit and underside of stairs drywalled per plan; no exposed framing in finished areas
Attic access hatches framed square, trimmed with J-bead, and sized per plan (minimum 22"x30" per IRC 807.1)
All tub and shower surrounds behind tile substrate confirmed: cement board or glass-mat gypsum (no standard drywall within wet area boundary per IRC R702.4)

categories.name: Inspections & Sign-Off
items: Baldwin County insulation inspection passed (if required as standalone inspection in jurisdiction) — card signed and on file
Superintendent final walk of all hung surfaces complete: flatness check with 8-foot straightedge, no deviation greater than 1/8" over 8 feet on ceiling planes
Drywall sub foreman sign-off on hang completion form — date and room-by-room confirmation recorded in project log
Superintendent photographs all blocking and backing locations before tape begins (archived to project Drive folder for warranty reference)
Any damaged, wet, or out-of-plane panels flagged, listed on punch sheet, and replaced before tape crew mobilizes
PM or superintendent notifies tape crew and schedules start date — no tape crew on site until this phase checklist is fully closed
